headlines.text: Trump threatens to restart bombing if Iran doesn't sign peace memo within days
US and Iran closing in on 1-page deal to end war, sources say
Stocks surge to new records as Iran deal hopes send oil tumbling
AMD jumps 16%, Disney up 8%, Uber up 6% on blowout earnings
Gas prices hit $4.54 national average, up 50% since the war started
Trump-backed challengers oust Indiana senators who blocked redistricting
RFK Jr. announces plan to curb psychiatric drug prescribing nationwide
Hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship prompts evacuation standoff at Canary Islands
Beyonce's Met Gala skeleton dress ignites Act III album fan theory firestorm
Apple in talks with Intel and Samsung to make US-built iPhone chips

breaking.text: BREAKING: Trump warns Iran 'the bombing starts' at higher intensity if no deal reached today

stories.hed: Peace Memo or More Bombs: Trump Gives Iran an Ultimatum
stories.dek: The US and Iran are reportedly within inches of signing a 1-page, 14-point memo to end the war, but Trump reminded everyone he has a plan B that involves considerably more explosions. Iran, for its part, has not formally responded and is exchanging diplomatic messages through Pakistan, which is really earning its mediator money right now.

stories.hed: Iran FM Meets China in Beijing as Diplomatic Scramble Intensifies
stories.dek: Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi traveled to Beijing Wednesday for his first China visit since the war began, meeting with Wang Yi as China urged a comprehensive ceasefire. It is the diplomatic equivalent of calling your richest friend when the landlord is at the door.

stories.hed: Trump Purges Indiana Republicans Who Defied Him on Redistricting
stories.dek: Five Trump-backed primary challengers defeated Indiana state senators who had voted against his mid-decade redistricting push. The message to Republican legislators everywhere was delivered clearly and without ambiguity: play ball or get the ball.

stories.hed: Senate GOP Tucks $1 Billion for Trump Ballroom Into Immigration Bill
stories.dek: Senate Judiciary Republicans included roughly $1 billion in Secret Service security upgrades tied to Trump's White House ballroom project inside a $72 billion immigration enforcement reconciliation package. Washington, where 'ballroom security' is now a line item.

stories.hed: Rubio Said the War Was Over. Trump Said It Wasn't. Both Were Speaking Simultaneously.
stories.dek: At the exact moment Secretary of State Rubio told reporters Operation Epic Fury was 'concluded,' Trump told PBS the combat operations were absolutely not over. The two statements were made at the same time from different rooms, which is either a communications breakdown or performance art.

markets.context: Markets hit new records and oil cratered as reports of a US-Iran peace memo sent traders into full risk-on mode, with AMD, Disney, and Uber earnings adding fuel to the rally.

gas.note: Up 50% since Feb 28 — turns out wars are bad for your commute and your wallet simultaneously.

popCulture.hed: Beyonce Returns to Met Gala After 10 Years in a Crystal Skeleton Gown, Breaks the Internet and Possibly Hints at Act III
popCulture.dek: Queen Bey co-chaired the 2026 Met Gala on May 4 wearing an Olivier Rousteing skeleton dress that sent the Beyhive into a frenzy over Act III album clues. She brought Blue Ivy as her date, because when you end a 10-year Met hiatus, you do it in full family armor.

genZ.definition: With The Devil Wears Prada 2 hitting theaters this week, TikTok FYPs have been flooded with creators using Miranda Priestly audio clips to roast everything from bad roommates to overpriced gas. The trend rewards maximum theatricality and a very specific kind of devastated stare.
genZ.usage: POV: you ask Trump and Rubio to get their war story straight and they give you two completely different answers at the same time. *Miranda Priestly stare*

tldr: The US and Iran are reportedly days away from a 1-page peace deal, sending stocks to record highs and oil tumbling, while Trump reminded everyone he is perfectly prepared to resume bombing if the paperwork falls through. Meanwhile, Beyonce wore a skeleton to the Met Gala, gas costs $4.54 a gallon, and somehow AMD had a better week than everyone.
